Wade buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for drinks is [drink_cost] * [number_of_drinks] = [total_drink_cost]. If Wade spends a total of [total_spent], then the amount spent on sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost]. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is ([total_spent] - [total_drink_cost]) / [number_of_sandwiches]. 

***

The lab [lab] is an important part of the calculation process. The total spent by Wade is [total_spent], and the total cost for drinks is [total_drink_cost]. The remaining amount, which is [remaining_amount], is spent on sandwiches. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is [cost_per_sandwich]. This calculation ensures that all expenses are accounted for and the cost per sandwich is determined accurately.
